—This chapter is a psalm of praise; we find it later nearly as . Those who trust God in the way of duty will find him a present help in their greatest dangers: David did so. Remarkable preservations should be particularly mentioned in our praises. We will never be delivered from all enemies until we get to heaven. God will preserve all his people. Those who receive significant mercies from God ought to give him the glory. In the day that God delivered David, he sang this song. While the mercy is fresh, and we are most affected by it, let the thank-offering be brought, kindled with the fire of that affection. All his joys and hopes close, as all our hopes should do, in the great Redeemer.
